field.label: License Portal URL
field.comment: URL of the eTechFlow license portal used to validate SP-XXXX keys. Default: https://license-service.etechflow.com

field.label: License Validation API URL (optional override)
field.comment: Optional server-to-server validation URL override. If blank, the Portal URL above is used.

field.label: License Key
field.tooltip: Paste your purchase-email key here. Bound to the production domain.
field.comment:
Paste the key from your purchase email. Tied to your live website's address.
Test or staging sites don't need a key — the module detects them automatically.
www. doesn't matter — one key works whether your address is shop.com or www.shop.com.

field.label: Enable Module
field.tooltip: Master on/off. When No, the Part Finder widget and vehicle filters do not render.
field.comment:
Master on/off switch.
Yes = the Part Finder widget renders and vehicle filters apply to catalog browsing.
No = the module does nothing on the storefront — useful for quickly disabling without uninstalling.

field.label: Earliest Year
field.tooltip: The oldest year that appears in the Year dropdown. Default 1990.
field.comment:
The oldest year that appears in the Year dropdown on the Part Finder.
Default 1990 covers modern cars.
Set to 1950 for vintage car parts shops (classic Mustangs, Land Rover Series, etc.).
Set to 2007 for smartphone-fitment shops where there's no point listing pre-iPhone years.
Anything below 1900 or above the current year is clamped to safe bounds.

field.label: Show Year Field
field.tooltip: Hide the Year dropdown if your fitment domain doesn't need it (phone cases, watches, etc.).
field.comment:
Show or hide the Year dropdown in the Part Finder form.
Yes (default) = Year dropdown renders — for any time-bound fitment (vehicles, electronics with model years, etc.).
No = Year dropdown is hidden — the form becomes Make → Model → Part. Useful for phone cases, watch straps, printer cartridges, appliance parts and similar domains where year doesn't matter.

field.label: "Make" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Make dropdown. Leave blank for the default ("Make").
field.comment:
Customer-facing label for the Make dropdown (top of the Part Finder).
Default: Make. Common alternatives:
- Brand — for phone cases, watches, appliances
- Manufacturer — for industrial parts
- Category — for cross-product fitment
Leave blank to use the default.

field.label: "Model" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Model dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Model. Common alternatives:
- Phone / Tablet — for accessories
- Watch — for watch parts
- Model Number — for industrial parts

field.label: "Year" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Year dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Year. Common alternatives:
- Generation — for phones (iPhone 13/14/15…)
- Year of Manufacture — for older fitments
- Model Year — for cars where it matters

field.label: "Parts" Field Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the Parts Required dropdown.
field.comment:
Default: Parts Required. Common alternatives:
- Type / Style — for phone cases
- Strap Size — for watch straps
- Component — for industrial parts

field.label: Show Fitment Badge on Product Page
field.tooltip: Yes = render a "Fits: BMW 3 Series 2018-2023" badge under the price on every PDP that has fitment data.
field.comment:
Renders a coloured "Fits: …" block right under the product price on the PDP for any product tagged with vehicle compatibility data.

Yes = badge renders when product has fitment data.
No (default) = no badge. Existing v1.0.x installs see no change unless they opt in.

field.label: Badge Prefix Text
field.tooltip: Bold label before the list of compatible vehicles. Default "Fits:".
field.comment:
Bold text shown before the vehicle list. Default Fits:. Alternatives:
- "Compatible with:" — formal
- "Made for:" — friendly
- "Bilingual stores": translate via Magento i18n.

field.label: Badge Style
field.tooltip: Visual treatment: success (green), info (blue), warning (amber), neutral (grey).
field.comment:
Success (green) — default, reads as a positive "yes this fits"
Info (blue) — neutral informational tone
Warning (amber) — attention-grabbing
Neutral (grey) — minimal

field.label: Enable SEO URLs
field.tooltip: Yes = also accept /parts/bmw/3-series/2020/brake-pads style URLs.
field.comment:
Adds a custom router that maps SEO-friendly paths to the Part Finder:
/parts/bmw/3-series/2020/brake-pads
instead of the default
/vehiclecompat/find/index?make=42&model=87&year=2020&part=12

Better SEO. Better social-share previews. Better crawlability.

Yes = both old and new URLs work — old links don't break.
No (default) = only the query-string URLs work. Existing v1.0.x installs see no change unless they opt in.

field.label: URL Prefix
field.tooltip: The first segment of the SEO URL. Default "parts". Use "fitment", "compat", "for", etc.
field.comment:
The first segment after the domain. Default: parts → /parts/bmw/3-series/…
Common alternatives:
- fitment → /fitment/bmw/…
- for → /for/bmw/… (very short, reads naturally)
- compatibility → longer but explicit

Sanitised to lowercase a–z, 0–9, and dashes. Anything else is stripped. Invalid values fall back to parts.

group.label: Customer-Facing Copy (v1.1.1)
group.comment: Override the customer-facing button text, page title, and empty-state messages. Pairs with the v1.0.2 label config so the entire Part Finder UX can be rebranded for non-vehicle domains (phone cases, watches, appliance parts, etc.). Leave blank to fall back to defaults.

field.label: Find Button Text
field.tooltip: Text on the big call-to-action button. Default "Find Parts".
field.comment:
Text on the big call-to-action button at the bottom of the Part Finder form. Default: Find Parts. Alternatives:
- Find Cases — phone case shops
- Find Watches — watch shops
- Find Filters — automotive filter shops
- Search — minimalist
Mixed-case recommended — v1.1.0 shipped all-caps "FIND PARTS" which read shouty.

field.label: Find Page Title
field.tooltip: H1 heading on the search results page. Default "Find Your Parts".
field.comment: H1 heading on the search results page. Default: Find Your Parts. Phone-case shop: Find Your Case. Watch shop: Find Your Strap. Industrial: Component Finder.

field.label: Empty State Message
field.tooltip: Message shown when no filters are active. Supports {make} {model} {year} {part} placeholders that expand to your configured labels.
field.comment:
Message shown when the customer lands on the Find page without any filters active. Supports four placeholders that automatically expand to your configured field labels (set in the General Settings group above):
{make} · {model} · {year} · {part}

Default: Pick a {make}, {model}, {year} or {part} to see matching products.
If you rename Make → Brand and Model → Phone, the empty state automatically reads Pick a Brand, Phone, Year or Part to see matching products. — no separate edit needed unless you want different wording.

field.label: Save Selection Button Text
field.tooltip: Text on the "save to garage" button next to Find. Default "Save Selection".
field.comment: Text on the save-to-garage button next to Find. Default: Save Selection. Renders only when the Customer Garage is enabled (below) and the customer has picked at least a Make.

field.label: Garage Empty-State Prompt
field.tooltip: Friendly nudge shown in the My Garage widget when no vehicles are saved.
field.comment:
Friendly prompt shown in the My Garage widget when the customer hasn't saved anything yet. Default: Save a selection here for one-click reload later.
Without this prompt the widget would render nothing on empty state, and customers would never discover the feature.

group.label: OEM / Part-Number Search (v1.2.0)
group.comment: Adds a "Or search by part number" input on the Find page so customers can paste an OEM / MPN number and find matching products directly (in addition to the Make/Model/Year/Part cascade).

field.label: Enable OEM Search
field.tooltip: Yes = customers can paste a part number into a search box on the Find page.
field.comment:
Yes = an "Or search by part number" input renders on the Find page. Customer pastes an OEM/MPN code, the catalog filters to products whose configured attribute(s) LIKE-match the term.
No (default) = no input renders. The `?oem=...` query param is also ignored.

field.label: Attribute Codes to Search
field.tooltip: Comma-separated product attribute codes. Default "sku".
field.comment:
Which product attributes the OEM search filters against. Comma-separated attribute codes — accepts a–z, 0–9, underscore only.
Default: sku
Common alternatives if you have custom attributes:
- sku, mpn
- sku, manufacturer_part_number, custom_oem
Each code becomes an OR clause; the customer's term matches if it appears in ANY of the configured attributes.

field.label: Search Box Label
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label above the search box. Default "Or search by part number".
field.comment:
Label rendered above the OEM search input. Default: Or search by part number.
Phone-case shops: "Or search by model number".
Watch shops: "Or search by reference number".

field.label: Search Box Placeholder
field.tooltip: Placeholder text inside the input field. Default "Type part number…".

group.label: Storefront Copy Polish + Tooltips (v1.2.1)
group.comment: Override the remaining customer-visible strings that v1.1.1 didn't expose — empty states, dropdown placeholders, garage actions, no-results messaging. Plus three customer-facing tooltips (rendered as title="") that give shoppers a hover hint on key UI elements. And a single accent colour override so the module's filter chips + buttons match your brand.

field.label: "No Results" Title
field.comment: Shown when filters return zero products. Default: "No products match all your filters."

field.label: "No Results" Hint
field.comment: Secondary line under the no-results title. Default: "Try removing one or two filters — fewer constraints usually find a match."

field.label: "Use the Form" Prompt
field.comment: Secondary line in the empty-state. Default: "Use the form above to start."

field.label: Dropdown "No Matches"
field.comment: Shown when a typed search yields no items in a dropdown. Default: "No matches"

field.label: Dropdown Search Placeholder
field.comment: Placeholder in each dropdown's search input. Default: "Search…"

field.label: PDP Fitment Badge Overflow
field.comment: When a product fits more than 3 vehicles, the badge shows a "+N more" marker. Default: and {count} more. Supports the {count} placeholder. Phone shop: "and {count} other phones".

field.label: Garage Widget Title
field.comment: Default: "My Garage". Watch shop: "My Watches". Phone shop: "My Phones".

field.label: Garage Clear-All Button
field.comment: Default: "Clear Garage". Watch shop: "Clear All Watches".

field.label: Garage Remove Button (aria-label)
field.comment: Tooltip for the "X" remove button. Default: "Remove"

field.label: "Saved!" Confirmation Text
field.comment: Shown for ~1.5s after a successful save-to-garage. Default: "Saved!"

field.label: Sidebar "No Filters" Message
field.comment: Shown in the Find page sidebar when no filters are picked. Default: "No filters active."

field.label: OEM Search Submit Button
field.comment: Text on the submit button next to the OEM search input. Default: "Search"

field.label: PDP Fitment Badge Tooltip
field.tooltip: Optional hover tooltip on the "Fits:" badge.
field.comment:
Hover tooltip (HTML title="") on the PDP fitment badge.
Optional. Leave blank to render no tooltip. Suggested text:
"This list shows the vehicles this part fits. Match your car's Make, Model and Year to confirm."
Phone shop: "Compatible with the phones listed. Match your phone model to confirm fit."

field.label: My Garage Tooltip
field.tooltip: Optional hover tooltip on the My Garage widget title.
field.comment:
Hover tooltip on the My Garage widget. Suggested:
"Vehicles you've saved for quick reload. Click any vehicle to filter the catalog by that fit. Saved per browser; sync'd across devices when logged in."

field.label: OEM Search Tooltip
field.tooltip: Optional hover tooltip on the OEM search input field.
field.comment:
Hover tooltip on the OEM search box. Suggested:
"Paste a part number (OEM, MPN, or your store's SKU) to find the matching product directly. Faster than clicking through the vehicle dropdowns if you already know the number."

field.label: Accent Colour (hex)
field.tooltip: Hex colour for filter chips + buttons. Default #0535F5 (eTechFlow blue).
field.comment:
Six-digit hex colour used for filter chips on the Find page, the OEM Search submit button, and Garage links. Default: #0535F5 (eTechFlow blue).
Set to match your brand. Examples:
- #16A34A (green)
- #DC2626 (red)
- #7C3AED (purple)
Anything that doesn't parse as a 6-digit hex falls back to the default. Surfaces via a CSS custom property --etechflow-vc-accent so your theme can also override it in CSS.

field.label: Enable Customer Garage
field.tooltip: Yes = customers can save their vehicle for one-click reload across sessions.
field.comment:
Adds a "My Garage" feature where customers can save the vehicle they picked in the Part Finder. Next time they visit, their vehicles are already there — one click reloads the Part Finder with that vehicle. Top-3 conversion driver for parts e-commerce.

v1.1.0 MVP: localStorage-based (per-browser). Guest + logged-in customer get the same experience.
v1.2.0+: customer attribute storage for logged-in users (syncs across devices).

Merchants place the widget where they want via layout XML or CMS block:
{{block class="ETechFlow\VehicleCompat\Block\Garage" template="ETechFlow_VehicleCompat::garage/widget.phtml"}}

No (default) = widget renders nothing even if placed.

field.label: Maximum Vehicles per Customer
field.tooltip: How many vehicles each customer can save. Default 3. Clamped 1-10.
field.comment:
How many vehicles each customer can save. Default 3.
Anything below 1 or above 10 is clamped to safe bounds. Most stores find 3 is the sweet spot — covers "my car, my wife's car, my work van".
